titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NLS Adv. MS 15.1.18, no. 12

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=98 status=live modified=2022-01-30 04:44:05.635123+00 | translation moa=99 status=live modified=2022-01-30 04:44:24.040697+00

p.cl: Robertus dei gratia humilis minister ecclesie sancti Andree . uniuersis sancte matris ecclesie filiis salutem

Innotescat tam posteris quam presentibus
nos dedisse & in liberam elemosinam concessisse deo & ecclesie sancti Andree & canonicis ibi deo seruientibus kininmuned cum toft de
Kilrimund & cum omnibus aliis appendiciis suis . Quapropter uolumus & precipimus quatinus ipsi hanc elemosinam ita
libere . & honorifice teneant ut decet elemosinam teneri .

Et si quis de hac elemosina iniuriam ecclesie sancti ANDREE & pre
dictis fratribus intulerit  uinculo anathematis constringatur . nisi digne satis fecerit .

T . Herberto episcopo Glasguensi . Willelmo
episcopo MVR’ . Arnaldo abbate de Chelc’ . Gaufrido abbate . de Dunf’ . Willelmo Abbate de Sancta Cruce . Ysaac abbate de striuel’ . Matheo archidiacono . Thor archidiacono . Aiulfo decano . Nicholaio clerico regis . Willelmo capellano episcopi Magistro ANDREA . Magistro herberto . Iohanne nepote episcopi . Radulfo nepote episcopi .

div.p: Robert, by the grace of God humble servant of the church of St Andrews, to the sons of holy mother Church everywhere, Greeting: Let it be known both to those to come as well as to those present that we have given, and granted in free alms to God and to the church of St Andrews and to the canons serving God there, Kinninmonth with the toft of Kilrymont and with all its other appurtenances. Wherefore we will and enjoin that they shall hold this alms as freely and honourably as alms ought to be held. And if anyone should bring injury to the church of St Andews and to the aforesaid brethren, let him be bound with the bond of anathema, unless he makes proper satisfaction. As witnesses: Herbert the bishop of Glasgow, William the bishop of Moray, Arnold the abbot of Kelso, Geoffrey the abbot of Dunfermline, William the abbot of Holyrood, Isaac the abbot of Stirling, Matthew the archdeacon, Thor the archdeacon, Aiulf the dean, Nicholas the king's clerk, William the bishop's chaplain, Master Andrew, Master Herbert, John the bishop's nephew, Ralph the bishop's nephew.
